title: On the Regulation of Multinational Banks
abstract: Countries can bene…t from the recent di¤usion of multinational enterprises in banking sector through increased competition and e¢ciency. However, these bene…ts may fail to realize if adequate supervision is not put in place for multinational banks (MNBs). Banks have become more global, but their regulators have remained national. In this paper we analyze the speci…c regulatory issues brought in by MNBs with particular attention to solvency and prudential regulation. We show the relevance of limited information national regulators may have on MNBs ’ activities and the need for information sharing. We address the issue of con‡icting national regulatory objectives and the consequence this may have on the control over MNBs. Finally, we argue that the (few) existing regulatory answers to these problems appear to be unsatisfactory.
